---
title: "STA3041F 2026 Tutorial Assessment 3"
author: "Amara Patel (PTLAMA005) and Malik Jamie (JMXMAL003)"
date: '2026-03-27'
output: pdf_document
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{pdfpages}
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Question 1

```{r, fig.align='center',fig.width=5,fig.height=5, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Figure caption here.'}
rm(list = ls())
dat = read.table("BatRun Data 2.txt", h = TRUE)
# head(dat) check the data if you like. 

# Plot the count trajectory and place tick marks for the arrivals:

```

## Question 2
\begin{equation*}
\begin{aligned}
\mbox{mvf}(v;\boldsymbol\beta) &= \int \lambda(u;\boldsymbol\beta)du\\
 &=\\
 &=\\
 &=\\
 &=\\ 
 &=K(\beta_2)\times\Phi\bigg(v;?,?\bigg)\\
\end{aligned}
\end{equation*}

## Question 3

```{r}


mvf_1    = function(v,pars)
{
		beta = pars
	  return()
}
min_likelihood_1 = function(pars)
{
	  beta  = pars
	  value =  
	  return(-value)
}

mvf_2    = function(v,pars)
{
	  beta  = 
	  K     = 
	  return()
}

min_likelihood_2 = function(pars)
{
	  beta  = 
	  value =  
	  return(-value)
}

```

## Question 4

```{r}
res_opt_1 = nlm()
res_opt_2 = nlm()
M1  = c()#beta hat here, #AIC here
M2  = c()#beta hat here, #AIC here
tab = cbind(M1,M2)
rownames(tab) = c('beta[1]','beta[2]','AIC')
colnames(tab) = c('Model 1','Model 2')
tab
```

## Question 5
```{r fig.align='center',fig.width=5,fig.height=5}

tt = 
E1 = 
E2 = 

```



## Question 6

```{r}
forecast = 
forecast
```
\newpage
\includepdf[pages=1]{Department of Statistical Sciences - Plagiarism Declaration.pdf}
